events.note: Google signed 32-month contract with SpaceX/xAI for ~110,000 NVIDIA GPUs hosted at xAI data centers (Oct 2026–Jun 2029). Bridge capacity cited for unexpected Gemini Enterprise demand surge. Terminable with 90-day notice after Dec 31, 2026. Notable inversion: a hyperscaler (Google) as the compute buyer, not the supplier.

events.event: FinOps X 2026 opens — San Diego (runs Jun 8–11)
events.note: Annual FinOps Foundation conference. Key outputs: Linux Foundation launched Tokenomics Foundation — open standards body for AI infrastructure economics (token pricing, GPU cost benchmarks, inference standards). Google Cloud launched Spend Caps and AI Explainability Agent. AWS announced AWS FinOps Agent. ~25% of sessions covered AI economics and token pricing. FOCUS 1.3 (ratified Dec 2025) confirmed as active spec — adds Contract Commitment dataset, expanded SaaS/PaaS scope.

SpaceX listed on Nasdaq raising ~$75B. Second pure-play AI compute infrastructure public name after CoreWeave (CRWV). Google–SpaceX compute deal announced June 5 — one week before listing. With CRWV and now SPCX, a publicly-traded AI infrastructure category is forming.

events.event: Synergy Research Q1 2026 cloud data — $129B, +35% YoY
events.note: Enterprise cloud spending hit $129B in Q1 2026 — ninth consecutive quarter of growth and highest growth rate since Q4 2021. AWS 28% share, Microsoft 21%, Google 14%. Q2 2026 data expected in coming weeks.

events.event: Anthropic × Google × Broadcom TPU financing close confirmed
events.note: 3.5 GW TPU deal (announced Apr 2026) reached financing milestone in early June: Apollo Global Management + Blackstone closed private credit package via SPV that purchases Google TPUs and leases back to Anthropic. Infrastructure comes online starting 2027. Anthropic run-rate revenue now ~$30B (up from ~$9B end of 2025).

events.event: Big 4 hyperscaler FY2026 CapEx confirmed ~$725B combined
events.note: Updated full-year guidance: Amazon ~$200B, Alphabet up to $190B (raised ~$5B from prior guidance), Microsoft ~$190B, Meta $115–135B. Up from ~$700B prior estimates. Analyst forecasts now project 71% YoY surge in hyperscaler AI investment for 2026. Q2 2026 earnings (late Jul/early Aug) may revise further upward.

events.event: NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 results current — Data Center $75.2B (+92% YoY)
events.note: NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 (ended Apr 2026, reported May 2026): Total revenue $81.6B (+85% YoY). Data Center segment $75.2B (+92% YoY, +21% QoQ). Q2 FY2027 guidance ~$91B. Next NVIDIA earnings ~Aug 2026.

events.event: Q2 2026 hyperscaler earnings window — watch for CapEx revisions
events.note: All four Big Tech companies expected to report late July–early August. Watch: FY2026 CapEx revisions (current combined guidance ~$725B), AI demand commentary, compute constraint updates, cloud segment growth rates vs Q1 (+35% YoY baseline). NVIDIA Q2 FY2027 (~$91B guidance) also in this window.
